description: Classify hedge fund strategies (equity hedge, event-driven, relative value, opportunistic macro) and describe their return drivers, risks, and typical market-environment suitability

equity_hedge.long_short_equity: Long undervalued / short overvalued equities; variable net exposure
equity_hedge.market_neutral: Zero market beta; alpha from stock selection
equity_hedge.short_biased: Net short; thrives in declining markets

event_driven.merger_arbitrage: Long target, short acquirer; profit on deal completion spread
event_driven.distressed: Long debt/equity of troubled issuers; restructuring profit
event_driven.activist: Take stake and force strategic/operational change

relative_value.fixed_income_arbitrage: Exploit mis-pricings in yield curves and credit spreads
relative_value.convertible_arbitrage: Long convertible bond / short stock; vol and credit decomposition

opportunistic_macro.global_macro: Top-down bets on rates, FX, equity indices, commodities
opportunistic_macro.managed_futures_cta: Systematic trend following across asset classes

risk_profile.leverage_usage: Relative value strategies often highly levered
risk_profile.tail_risk: Event-driven and credit strategies exposed to default cycles

classify_strategy.description: Classify hedge fund by strategy family

errors.message: strategy_family must be equity_hedge, event_driven, relative_value, or opportunistic

anti_patterns.rule: Do not rely on historical Sharpe ratios for relative-value funds
anti_patterns.why: Smooth returns in benign periods hide tail risk; LTCM-style blow-ups are the defining risk of the strategy

anti_patterns.rule: Do not lump market-neutral and long-short equity together
anti_patterns.why: Market-neutral aims for zero beta; long-short can have 30-60% net exposure — very different risk profiles

anti_patterns.rule: Do not ignore capacity constraints in managed futures and vol arb
anti_patterns.why: Strategies crowd out as AUM scales; live Sharpe typically falls materially below back-tested
